title: Add type checking for $countones
body:
## Summary
Currently, Verilator does not catch type mismatches for `$countones` during the Verilate step. The issue only surfaces during the C++ compilation step, which can make debugging more challenging. It would be helpful if this type of issue could be identified during Verilation.

## Versions
- Verilator 5.031 devel rev v5.030-144-g54ef9ad31 (latest)
- OS: WSL Ubuntu 24.04

## Steps to Reproduce

Code snippet:

```systemverilog
module Test;

  logic my_vec [4];
  int count_ones;

  initial begin
    my_vec = '{1, 0, 1, 0};
    count_ones = $countones(my_vec);
    $display("Number of ones in my_vec is %d", count_ones);
  end

endmodule
```

Commands to run:
```
verilator test.sv --cc --exe --binary --main
```

Error during C++ compilation:

```
Vtest___024root__DepSet_hc07518e4__0__Slow.cpp:39:54: error: cannot convert ‘VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 4>’ to ‘IData’ {aka ‘unsigned int’}
   39 |     Test__DOT__count_ones = VL_COUNTONES_I(vlSelfRef.Test__DOT__my_vec);
      |                                            ~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                                                      |
      |                                                      VlUnpacked<unsigned char, 4>
```

## Expected Behavior
It would be great if Verilator could report an error during the Verilate step, such as:
```
Error: $countones requires a scalar or packed array, but an unpacked array 'my_vec' was provided.
```

This would make it easier to identify and resolve the issue early in the design flow.
